cefr.label: CEFR
cefr.summary: A practical CEFR-style route into word-order practice.

levels.label: A1
levels.summary: Concrete classroom language, be, have, can, and present simple.
levels.focus: Concrete classroom language, be, have, can, and present simple.
hints: Look for be, have, or can.

levels.label: A2
levels.summary: Past simple, going to, reasons, comparisons, and classroom routines.
levels.focus: Past simple, going to, reasons, comparisons, and classroom routines.

levels.label: B1
levels.summary: Conditionals, present perfect, modals, relative clauses, and clear opinions.
levels.focus: Conditionals, present perfect, modals, relative clauses, and clear opinions.

levels.label: B2
levels.summary: Passive voice, reported speech, contrast, purpose, and clause control.
levels.focus: Passive voice, reported speech, contrast, purpose, and clause control.

levels.label: C1
levels.summary: Inversion, hedging, nominalisation, cleft sentences, and academic phrasing.
levels.focus: Inversion, hedging, nominalisation, cleft sentences, and academic phrasing.

myp.label: MYP
myp.summary: MYP-flavoured sentence sets using inquiry, global contexts, concepts, and classroom reflection.

levels.label: Phase 1-2
levels.summary: Identity, community, classroom objects, simple description, and early inquiry language.
levels.focus: Identity, community, classroom objects, simple description, and early inquiry language.
hints: Find who or what the sentence is about.

levels.label: Phase 2-3
levels.summary: Past events, simple reasons, comparisons, communities, and global contexts.
levels.focus: Past events, simple reasons, comparisons, communities, and global contexts.

levels.label: Phase 3-4
levels.summary: Perspectives, evidence, relative clauses, action, and inquiry reflection.
levels.focus: Perspectives, evidence, relative clauses, action, and inquiry reflection.

levels.label: Phase 4-5
levels.summary: Key concepts, related concepts, reported ideas, passive voice, and purpose.
levels.focus: Key concepts, related concepts, reported ideas, passive voice, and purpose.

levels.label: Phase 5-6
levels.summary: Nuanced conceptual language, global contexts, hedging, synthesis, and academic claims.
levels.focus: Nuanced conceptual language, global contexts, hedging, synthesis, and academic claims.

tefl.label: TEFL
tefl.summary: General English classroom bands for warmer, filler, revision, and fluency lessons.

levels.label: Beginner
levels.summary: Everyday nouns, routines, be, have, can, and simple present.
levels.focus: Everyday nouns, routines, be, have, can, and simple present.

levels.label: Elementary
levels.summary: Past events, future plans, reasons, quantities, and comparisons.
levels.focus: Past events, future plans, reasons, quantities, and comparisons.

levels.label: Pre-Intermediate
levels.summary: Advice, experiences, preferences, reasons, and short clauses.
levels.focus: Advice, experiences, preferences, reasons, and short clauses.

levels.label: Intermediate
levels.summary: Connected clauses, conditions, relative clauses, purpose, and reported ideas.
levels.focus: Connected clauses, conditions, relative clauses, purpose, and reported ideas.
hints: Track the subject of each clause.

levels.label: Upper-Intermediate
levels.summary: Passive voice, contrast, reported speech, and more complex time relationships.
levels.focus: Passive voice, contrast, reported speech, and more complex time relationships.

levels.label: Advanced
levels.summary: Inversion, hedging, emphasis, nominalisation, and academic argument.
levels.focus: Inversion, hedging, emphasis, nominalisation, and academic argument.
